name: GT-jordan-identity
source: HOS (Hanche-Olsen & Stormer, Jordan Operator Algebras), refs/hos/joa-m.md:825 --- eq (2.18), the EXACT weak associativity (special Jordan identity) for the symmetrized product of ANY associative algebra, VERBATIM: "$$a \circ (b \circ a^2) = (a \circ b) \circ a^2. \tag{2.18}$$". Here aob=(1/2)(ab+ba) (GT-jordan-product) is the symmetrized product of an associative product. Read this with the abstract associative product being the GENUINELY ASSOCIATIVE product of B(H): it is an EXACT identity (no error term) of elements, holding for the special Jordan product on any associative algebra. Used to certify that the eps=0 (exactly-associative) Jordan-identity defect vanishes, so the eps-C* defect is the only source of JB4 error.
